scope: Standalone branch review: feat/frontend-error-reporting vs merge-base a5dc969be49ab972d309d7de61916da4bf95cca6 (local main). 46 files.

applied_fixes.title: Literal NUL/0x1F control bytes embedded in a comment in frontend/src/lib/errors.ts corrupted the file to git-binary
applied_fixes.action: Replaced raw control bytes with the escaped text \x00-\x1f; file now diffs as text.

applied_fixes.title: Server-generated report id typed as number on the Vue side but the backend always returns a UUID string
applied_fixes.action: Changed all reportId/id type annotations from number to string across the Vue capture/notification/panel chain; updated test fixtures from numeric literals to string UUIDs.

applied_fixes.title: tests/test_webui_api_v1_error_reports.py mutates SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE without registering in the security-toggle-mutation-gate allowlist (confirmed CI-breaking)
applied_fixes.action: Added ("test_webui_api_v1_error_reports.py", "SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE") to GRANDFATHERED, matching the established pattern for sibling /api/v1 test files. Verified test_security_toggle_mutation_gate.py now only shows pre-existing, unrelated drift from test_webui_content_fetch_gate.py (confirmed via git-stash bisection to predate this branch).

applied_fixes.title: GET /api/v1/error-reports limit/offset pagination had zero test coverage
Fingerprint-merge 'row vanished' race fallback (increment_occurrence returns False) had zero test coverage
sendJson's new keepalive option was never asserted on by any test
applied_fixes.action: Added a paired true/false assertion in client.spec.ts and a direct assertion in errorCapture.spec.ts's existing happy-path test.

residual_actionable_work.title: retryBufferedReports() (legacy JS) has no in-flight guard -- overlapping visibilitychange/pagehide flushes can double-submit and drop buffered reports
Legacy ReportPanel._submit() has no busy/disabled guard -- a double-click fires two concurrent submissions
PATCH /api/v1/error-reports/<id> ignores attach_description()/update_status() boolean return values, producing a false-success 200 on a concurrent-delete race
increment_occurrence()/update_status()/attach_description() keep the SELECT-then-UPDATE lost-update race the module docstring only disclaims for add()
Request-size guard trusts Content-Length only -- absent/spoofed header (e.g. chunked transfer-encoding) bypasses the 100KB cap

Client-supplied fingerprint is trusted verbatim for merge/increment decisions -- any script can forge occurrence-count inflation on an arbitrary existing report (non-secret data, integrity-only impact)
residual_actionable_work.note: Design-level tradeoff, not a mechanical fix -- needs an explicit accept-or-fix decision.

residual_actionable_work.title: New /api/v1/error-reports surface (all 5 operations) was never added to the OpenAPI 3.1 contract (spec.py/schemas.py/openapi/backlink-api.yaml) unlike every sibling resource
Vue SPA capture engine (errorCapture.ts) never flushes DedupTracker.collectPendingUpdates(), silently losing in-window duplicate-occurrence counts the legacy JS engine reports via its periodic flush
Shared reportPanel Pinia store lets the sibling trigger retarget an open panel mid-submit, silently losing the other session's typed text
_enforce_daily_cap() fully JSON-decodes every report created today on every POST instead of SELECT COUNT(*)
Double as unknown as {...} cast in the resource-error branch bypasses the narrowed target type
notifications.ts push()'s 4th positional reportId param is positional-param creep, not an options object

advisory: reportId means two unrelated things (client correlation string vs. server row-id string) across adjacent modules -- naming ambiguity, no functional bug (kieran-typescript).
Layer-3 exact-secret masking has no minimum-length floor -- a very short operator-configured credential could over-redact ordinary substrings (adversarial, P3).
An uncaught Pinia action rejection can double-submit as two distinct rows because a context-prefixed message changes the fingerprint (adversarial, P2, needs verification).

allowJs:true in frontend/tsconfig.json is project-wide, not scoped to the one cross-boundary import; nothing prevents a future relative .js import elsewhere from silently skipping type-checking (kieran-typescript).
pre_existing_unrelated: test_webui_content_fetch_gate.py has its own unlisted security-toggle-mutation-gate drift (WTF_CSRF_ENABLED/SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE/CSRF_ENABLED) -- confirmed via git log to predate this branch entirely.
Origin-guard coverage snapshot has a previously-flagged, still-unresolved +2 pre-existing drift unrelated to Unit 3 (noted at Unit 3 commit time).

token-resolution.spec.ts and SideNav.spec.ts frontend test failures (color-token literals in HistoryPage.vue/KeepAlivePage.vue; a nav-items legacy-href assumption no longer true) -- confirmed present on an unmodified checkout before any of this plan's units touched anything.

462 unrelated pytest failures across the full backend suite (chmod/0600 Windows-incompatible assertions, unrelated route/redirect tests) -- confirmed zero overlap with any of the 10 Python files this plan touched; environment/platform-specific, not introduced by this branch.
learnings: All institutional learnings cited by the plan's own research phase (self-doc-sanitization-leak-recurrence, app-level-csrf-guard-makes-blueprint-csrf-dead-code, lite-accepted-deferrals R5, typed-error-envelope-over-stderr-truncation, negative-assertion-locks-in-bug) were verified as correctly applied, not violated.

coverage.suppressed_below_threshold: not separately tracked per-reviewer; each reviewer's own confidence gate applied
